name: Autumn Chicken'n' Dumplings
recipeIngredient: 4 large Leeks, cleaned & cut into 1-inch long slices
2 Apples, tart, cut into thick wedges
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large plastic or paper bag, coat chicken with flour in 2 batches, shaking off excess. Place the chicken in a single layer on wax paper.
2. Warm 3 tablespoons of olive oil in an 8-quart Dutch oven over medium heat. Add chicken in batches so the pan is not crowded. Cook for 15 to 20 minutes per batch, or until browned on all sides. Remove the chicken to a large plate.
3. Warm remaining 1 tablespoon of oil in the same pan over medium heat. Add leeks; cook for 5 minutes, or until lightly browned, stirring to scrape up brown bits. Add carrots, celery, and apple wedges; cook for 4 minutes. Add the browned chicken, broth, juice, dill, salt, and pepper. Cover; bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer for 15 minutes.
4. While the chicken cooks, make dumpling batter (recipe follows).
Dinner Plan: Before adding dumplings, remove half of the stew to a microwave-safe container and cool it. Then freeze for later use. With a large spoon, drop dumpling batter in 8 spoonfuls over the remaining stew. Simmer over medium-low heat, covered, about 16 minutes, or until dumplings are done. (Approximate microwave time: Defrost in the microwave for about 45 minutes. When defrosted, place in a deep, 10-inch skillet and make another batch of dumplings, continuing to cook as directed above.)
